$comment: Generated by Phase 2 (TODO-03) Joint Blueprint Validation. Cross-validation of PRD.md v2.0 + hld.md (APPROVED) + openapi.yaml/fr_api_traceability.json/error_catalog.json (APPROVED). CRITICAL findings are fixed in-session and do NOT appear here; only MEDIUM/LOW advisories are carried to Phase B. AC-ADV-* items are merged API-contract advisories.

advisories.finding: fr_api_traceability.json header scalars are internally inconsistent with the body. The header declares fr_mapped_to_api=19 and fr_non_api=10, but the mappings array actually contains 23 type=api and 6 type=non-api entries (=29 total), and non_api_summary.count=6. The substantive FR-to-operationId mapping is complete and correct (100% FR accountability holds); only the two summary scalars are stale. This is a documentation-accuracy defect in an otherwise-correct APPROVED artifact, not an architectural or coverage gap.
advisories.recommendation: Phase 1.5 owner (or first Phase B doc pass): set fr_mapped_to_api=23 and fr_non_api=6 in the header to match the mappings array and non_api_summary.count. Do not change any mapping entry. No openapi.yaml change is required. Validation left this upstream-APPROVED file untouched per the do-not-modify scope; the fix is a one-line scalar correction.

advisories.finding: deleteDocument (DELETE /v1/documents/{id}) declares 202/401/403/404/429/500 but no 503. The erasure cascade (OAQ-2) tombstones the Postgres documents row in a synchronous transaction before the async outbox sweep. If Postgres is unreachable at that synchronous step, the operation cannot tombstone and would fail; the error_catalog maps SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE to dependency-down scenarios but does not list deleteDocument among its operation_ids.
advisories.recommendation: Add a 503 ServiceUnavailable response to deleteDocument and append deleteDocument to SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE.operation_ids in error_catalog.json, so a Postgres-down erasure attempt returns a retryable 503 (with Retry-After) rather than a 500. Confirm the outbox sweep is idempotent on retry. This is additive and does not alter the erasure semantics.

advisories.finding: x-rate-limit annotation coverage is partial. It is present on routeQuery, answerQuery, and ingestDocument, but absent on listDocuments, getDocument, deleteDocument, getDocumentToc, and exportDocumentData even though all of those declare a 429 RATE_LIMITED response. NFR-015/security expects every authenticated operation to be rate-limited; the response is declared but the documented limit is missing on read/management endpoints.
advisories.recommendation: Add x-rate-limit blocks (or a documented default policy reference) to the five document-management/compliance operations so the documented limit matches the declared 429. A shared default (e.g. per-credential 60/minute) is sufficient for reads; tighten exportDocumentData and deleteDocument as sensitive operations.

advisories.finding: The router prompt-injection mitigation (strict-JSON output schema, reject non-JSON, system/role separation) is specified in HLD §8 / NFR-008 but has no machine-checkable contract artifact. The OpenAPI surface does not (and should not) expose the internal router prompt, so Phase B has no enforceable test hook for the OWASP-LLM01 control beyond prose.
advisories.recommendation: Phase B (Router Node) MUST implement output-schema validation against the RouterOutput contract (HLD §7.1) and reject any non-conforming LLM response with a deterministic fallback, and Phase D MUST add a prompt-injection fixture set (malicious section titles / queries) to the router golden tests. Treat untrusted document text + query as the injection surface.

advisories.finding: RouteResponse.estimated_token_reduction is typed as a string with pattern ^[0-9]{1,3}%$ (e.g. '87%'). A string-encoded percentage is harder for enterprise integrators (Priya persona) to consume programmatically for ROI/dashboards and cannot express fractional or >100% edge values cleanly. This matches the PRD example but is a weaker contract than a numeric field.
advisories.recommendation: Phase B may optionally add a numeric companion field (e.g. estimated_token_reduction_pct: number, 0-100) alongside the existing string for backward compatibility, or document that the string is display-only. Non-blocking; revisit when the FR-020 analytics dashboard is scheduled.

advisories.finding: FR-014 (multi-document routing) is exposed via optional RouteRequest.document_ids[] (OAQ-4 fan-out) on the routing-only enterprise endpoint, but AnswerRequest (personal tool) only accepts a single document_id. The PRD lists FR-014 as Phase 2 and does not state whether multi-document answering (not just routing) is in scope for the personal tool.
advisories.recommendation: Confirm with product that multi-document is routing-only for MVP (enterprise) and single-document for the personal-tool answer path; if multi-document answering is desired later, AnswerRequest will need a document_ids[] additive extension. Default assumption recorded: routing-only multi-doc for now. Aligns with OAQ-4 (MVP graph implements per-document; merge layer additive).

advisories.finding: RouteRequest uses both a oneOf (document_id | document_ids) and the VALIDATION_ERROR catalog entry already anticipates 'both/neither of document_id|document_ids'. However document_id is not in RouteRequest.required (only query is), and the oneOf enforces exactly-one-of the two. This is correct OpenAPI but some codegen/validator toolchains handle top-level oneOf on a request body inconsistently, risking a 422-vs-400 divergence across SDKs (FR-021).
advisories.recommendation: Phase B api-testing-engineer: add explicit contract tests asserting (a) document_id alone -> 200, (b) document_ids alone -> 200, (c) both present -> 422 VALIDATION_ERROR, (d) neither present -> 422 VALIDATION_ERROR. Pin the validator behaviour so generated SDKs agree. No schema change required.

advisories.finding: The SSE /v1/answer mid-stream error is delivered as an event: error carrying a Problem payload, but a 200 status has already been sent (stream opened). Clients that key error handling on HTTP status (rather than the SSE event name) could miss a mid-stream failure. This is inherent to SSE and documented, but is an integration footgun for the React client (ADR-9) and enterprise SDKs.
advisories.recommendation: Phase B react-engineer + SDK docs: explicitly document and test the mid-stream error contract (event: error -> Problem) so the personal-tool client surfaces it to the user and does not treat a 200-opened stream as guaranteed success. Add a streaming-failure fixture to Phase D.

cross_validation_summary.ba_fr_coverage: All 29 PRD FRs accounted for (23 api + 6 non-api). The 6 non-api FRs (FR-013, FR-015, FR-019, FR-020, FR-021, FR-022) are legitimately non-API (internal strategies or deferred P2). DPDP erasure (deleteDocument), access (exportDocumentData), no-retention (flags), residency (residency_region), and x-pii flagging are all present. Acceptance criteria (BDD §12) are implementable against the contract.
cross_validation_summary.pm_alignment: WSJF/MVP sequencing holds: P0 foundation FR-001..009 all API-mapped; P1 enterprise FR-010/FR-025..029 all mapped. x-pii and x-audit-log annotations present on all required (PII-bearing / write) operations; x-rate-limit coverage partial (ADV-003). GTM/tiers reflected in x-rate-limit scopes.
cross_validation_summary.sa_schema_to_hld: Every API schema entity (Document, Section/TocEntry, Citation, RelevantSection, chunk payload) maps to the HLD §4 data model (documents/sections/chunks). section_id is the universal join/filter key throughout. Error codes map to HLD fault-tolerance: SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE->fallback branch (NFR-013), FORBIDDEN->IDOR/tenant isolation (HLD §8), deleteDocument 202+ErasureReceipt->OAQ-2 outbox erasure. /v1/route routing-only and /v1/answer SSE invariants honored.
cross_validation_summary.consensus: JOINT APPROVED, 1 round, 0 CRITICAL open items. No HLD change required; hld.md stands. 8 advisories (3 MEDIUM, 5 LOW incl. 2 AC-ADV) carried to Phase B.
